---
title: "Classification I"
subtitle: "Logistic regression and generative models"
date: "Sep 25, 2026"
date-format: long
---

Logistic regression as a discriminative classifier; LDA, QDA, and naive Bayes as generative classifiers. Decision boundaries and what each approach assumes about the data.

📖 **Assigned reading:** ISLR ch. 4

---

## 📊 Slides

- 🖥️ [Clean slides](slides_clean.pdf)
- ✏️ [Annotated slides](slides_annotated.pdf)  *(in-class version)*

## 📚 Readings

- 📄 [Van Calster et al. (2025)](readings/Van Calster et al. (2025).pdf)

## 🧪 Lab

In-class walkthrough — open the rendered lab in a new tab. The R Markdown source is provided so you can run the code yourself in RStudio.

- 🧪 [class5 lab1 (HTML)](code/class5_lab1.html){target="_blank"}
- 🧪 [lab1 (HTML)](code/lab1.html){target="_blank"}
- 📝 [Lab source (lab1.Rmd)](code/lab1.Rmd)

## 💻 Code

**Figure scripts** — reproduce each figure from the slides:

- 📈 [fig1.R](code/fig1.R)
- 📈 [fig2.R](code/fig2.R)
- 📈 [fig3.R](code/fig3.R)
- 📈 [fig4.R](code/fig4.R)
- 📈 [fig5.R](code/fig5.R)

**Other scripts:**

- 📜 [extra_simulation.R](code/extra_simulation.R)

## 🗂️ Data

- 📊 [winequality.csv](data/winequality.csv)

## ⭐ Extras

Optional supplementary material:

- 📄 [Classification Recap](extras/classification_recap.pdf)
